name: Pico Mirrio Trail
name_original: Pico Mirrio – circular desde Feria hasta la Sierra Vieja
description: Hike the moderate 4.9-mile (7.8 km) Pico Mirrio circular trail from Feria to Sierra Vieja, gaining 1013 feet (309 metres) in about 2 hours a
region_intro: Hiking around Feria offers access to the distinctive landscapes of Extremadura, characterized by extensive dehesas of holm and cork oaks, rolling hills, and rocky terrain. The region features Mediterranean ecosystems, including scrubland and dramatic river valleys, with ancient mountains forming the backdrop. Prominent features for hikers include Pico Mirrio and the Sierra Vieja, which provide varied elevations and panoramic views. Best hiking trails around Feria The most popular hiking route is View of Feria and Pico Mirrio – View of Feria Castle loop from Feria, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers views of Feria Castle and the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Mirrio Trail, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail ascends Pico Mirrio, providing expansive views from its summit. Local hikers also love the View of Feria and Pico Mirrio loop from Feria, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through rolling hills and dehesas, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Feria is defined by dehesas, rolling hills, and rocky terrain, with routes traversing the Sierra Vieja and ascending Pico Mirrio. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Feria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Feria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Feria?
A: Feria offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 7 easy trails and 16 moderate options, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike around Feria?
A: The ideal seasons for hiking in Feria are spring and autumn. Spring transforms the landscape with a 'rainbow-colored wedge of wildflowers,' while autumn brings vibrant hues to the dehesa woodlands. Summers can be intense, so cooler months are generally preferred for comfortable exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Feria?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to experience the diverse scenery around Feria. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria and Pico Mirrio – View of Feria Castle loop from Feria&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 miles (11.6 km), and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19379148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria and Pico Mirrio loop from Feria&lt;/a&gt; at 3.7 miles (6.0 km). Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991603066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.1 miles (8.2 km).

Q: Are the hiking trails around Feria suitable for families?
A: Many trails around Feria are suitable for families, especially the 7 easy routes available. The region's varied terrain, including rolling hills and dehesas, offers engaging walks without extreme difficulty. It's always recommended to check the specific trail details for distance and elevation to match your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Feria?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not provided, generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Extremadura. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Feria?
A: Hiking in Feria immerses you in the distinctive Extremadura landscape. You'll encounter extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, semi-natural open woodlands dominated by holm and cork oaks, alongside rolling hills and rocky terrain. The region also features Mediterranean ecosystems, including scrubland and dramatic river valleys, with ancient mountains forming a scenic backdrop.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the trails?
A: Absolutely. A prominent feature is Pico Mirrio, which offers breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views from its 812-meter summit. From here, you can observe the picturesque Tierra de Barros, dotted with olive groves and meadows. Many trails also offer views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which crowns a nearby hill.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevations for hikes in Feria?
A: The hiking trails around Feria vary in length and elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991291225/pico-mirrio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mirrio Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 4.8 miles (7.8 km) with an elevation gain of approximately 308 meters. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria and Pico Mirrio – View of Feria Castle loop from Feria&lt;/a&gt; cover 7.2 miles (11.6 km) with about 493 meters of ascent. You'll find options ranging from shorter, flatter walks to more demanding ascents.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Feria?
A: The hiking routes in Feria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique natural environment, the rewarding panoramic views from Pico Mirrio, and the integration of cultural heritage with the natural landscape.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to see while hiking?
A: Yes, hiking around Feria offers glimpses into its rich historical context. The town itself is a declared Historic-Artistic Site, with whitewashed houses cascading down the slopes. You can also see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical landmark. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6458193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande in Zafra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3761785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of La Candelaria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What makes the Pico Mirrio area special for hiking?
A: Pico Mirrio is a highlight for hikers due to its challenging yet rewarding ascent and the spectacular 360-degree panoramic views from its 812-meter summit. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991291225/pico-mirrio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mirrio Trail&lt;/a&gt; traverse the Sierra Vieja, offering a unique natural environment within the northernmost foothills of Sierra Morena.
tips: Mirrio Peak in the Sierra Vieja is a popular hiking trail in Feria, Extremadura. It's a medium-difficulty hike that offers stunning panoramic views and a circular route from Feria.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Pico Mirrio – circular from Feria to the Sierra Vieja trail?
faqs.answer: According to komoot data, this circular hike is rated as moderate. While specific details about the terrain for this exact route are not available, moderate trails typically involve some elevation gain and varied surfaces, suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What is the distance and estimated duration of this hike?
faqs.answer: The Pico Mirrio – circular from Feria to the Sierra Vieja trail is approximately 7.8 kilometers (4.8 miles) long. It is estimated to take around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete, though individual times may vary based on pace and stops.

faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the start of this particular route is not available. We recommend checking local maps or resources for designated parking areas in Feria or near the Sierra Vieja trailhead before your visit.

faqs.answer: Information regarding whether dogs are allowed or specific leash rules for this exact trail is not available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead concerning pets before bringing your dog.

faqs.answer: Without specific local information for this trail, it's difficult to recommend an exact best time. Generally, for moderate hikes in similar regions, spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or winter cold. faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: We do not have specific information about permits or entrance fees for the Pico Mirrio – circular from Feria to the Sierra Vieja trail. Please consult local authorities or park information if this route passes through a protected area.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: Detailed terrain descriptions for this specific route are not available. However, given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and location, you might encounter varied surfaces, potentially including dirt paths, rocky sections, or forest trails. Always wear appropriate footwear.

faqs.answer: One notable point of interest along this route is the View of Feria and Pico Mirrio. This highlight offers scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For any moderate day hike, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended, especially if trail markers are sparse.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Mirrio – circular from Feria to the Sierra Vieja suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, which suggests it's not overly challenging, specific details for beginners are unavailable. If you are new to hiking, ensure you are comfortable with the distance and elevation gain, and consider starting with easier trails to build confidence.
